stories.headline: Robots Race Against Humans at Beijing Half Marathon, Winning Machine Beats Human World Record Pace
stories.summary:
A humanoid robot named Lightning, developed by Chinese smartphone maker Honor, won a half marathon race in Beijing on Sunday, completing the 21-kilometer course in 50 minutes and 26 seconds. The event featured dozens of robots competing alongside human runners in what organizers billed as a landmark demonstration of advances in robotics and autonomous movement.

Lightning's finishing time would beat the men's human half marathon world record of 57 minutes and 20 seconds, set by Uganda's Jacob Kiplimo in Lisbon in March, though the races are not directly comparable given differences in course conditions and competitive context. Approximately 40 percent of the competing robots ran autonomously, while the remainder were remotely controlled by operators.

The race drew international attention as a showcase of China's rapidly advancing robotics industry, with multiple technology companies fielding entries. The event highlighted the growing capabilities of bipedal robots in endurance tasks previously considered the exclusive domain of human athletes.

The competition is seen as part of China's broader push to establish itself as a global leader in robotics and artificial intelligence, sectors that have become central to the country's economic and strategic ambitions.

stories.headline: Remains of 50 Infants and 6 Adults Found Dumped at Trinidad Graveyard
stories.summary:
Police in Trinidad and Tobago have discovered the remains of at least 50 infants and six adults dumped at a graveyard in the town of Cumuto, approximately 40 kilometers from the capital Port of Spain. The Trinidad and Tobago Police Service issued a statement describing the discovery as potentially involving the "unlawful disposal of unclaimed corpses."

Of the six adult remains, five had identification tags attached, and two showed signs of having undergone post-mortem examinations, suggesting some may have originated from a medical or mortuary facility. Police commissioner Allister Guevarro called the discovery "deeply troubling" and said any individual or institution found to have violated its legal duties would be held fully accountable.

Authorities said it remains unclear whether the incident is connected to the country's significant gang violence problem. Trinidad and Tobago has one of the highest murder rates in Latin America and the Caribbean, and has been operating under a state of emergency since March 2 that grants police extended search and arrest powers.

The United States State Department has issued multiple travel advisories for Trinidad and Tobago in recent months, citing crime and a heightened risk of terrorism, though it noted that violent crime has dropped considerably since 2024 due to sustained security operations.

stories.headline: Loneliness Linked to Lower Memory Performance in Older Adults but Not Faster Decline, Study Finds
stories.summary:
A new study published in the journal Aging & Mental Health finds that loneliness is associated with poorer memory performance in older adults, but does not appear to accelerate the rate of memory decline over time. Researchers from Colombia, Spain, and Sweden analyzed data from more than 10,000 adults aged 65 to 94 across 12 European countries over a seven-year period.

Participants who reported higher levels of loneliness scored lower on both immediate and delayed memory tests at the start of the study. However, all groups, regardless of their reported loneliness levels, experienced similar rates of memory decline over the following seven years, a finding the lead author described as "surprising."

Lead researcher Dr. Luis Carlos Venegas-Sanabria of the Universidad del Rosario said the results suggest loneliness may play a more prominent role in the initial state of memory than in its progressive deterioration. About 8 percent of participants reported high loneliness levels at baseline; that group tended to be older, more likely female, and more likely to have conditions such as depression.

The findings contribute to ongoing scientific debate about whether loneliness is a direct risk factor for dementia. Prior research has produced mixed results, and the new study suggests the relationship between social isolation and cognitive health may be more nuanced than previously understood.

stories.headline: Elon Musk Answers Dying Teenager's Handwritten Questions Posthumously, Names Her Plush Design SpaceX Mascot
stories.summary:
Elon Musk publicly answered all eight questions left by a 15-year-old cancer patient, Liv Perrotto, who died before she could speak with him directly. Conservative commentator Glenn Beck shared Perrotto's story on X after her mother, Rebecca, provided him with a handwritten list of questions found on her nightstand following her death.

Perrotto had been given the opportunity to speak with Musk in her final days but was too exhausted to take the call and asked him to call back later. She died before that conversation could take place, leaving behind the handwritten list of questions covering topics ranging from Tesla product plans to his favorite anime and his travels to Japan.

Musk replied directly to Beck's post on Thursday, answering each question in order. Among his responses, he confirmed plans to expand the Tesla Diner, said his favorite anime is the film "Your Name," and noted he has visited Japan several times. He also agreed, with a simple "OK" and a smiley face, to name a plush toy Perrotto had designed for the Polaris Dawn space mission as an official SpaceX mascot.

The post drew nearly two million views and tens of thousands of likes on X. Perrotto's mother responded to Musk's answers with the message, "I wish she was here to see this."

stories.headline: One Dead, Three Injured After Woman Drives Into Group During Parking Lot Disturbance in Dallas
stories.summary:
Dallas police responded early Friday to a disturbance involving two groups at a business in the 3300 block of East Trinity Mills Road, where a woman struck multiple people with her vehicle in the parking lot. Officers were called to the scene around 4:20 a.m. following a disturbance emergency call requesting an ambulance.

Kiara Lewis, 32, was transported to a hospital in critical condition and later pronounced dead. Three other individuals — two women and one man — were also hospitalized but were subsequently released after treatment.

According to investigators, the altercation began inside the business and continued outside into the parking lot, at which point a woman got into her car and drove into the group. The name of the driver and any potential charges had not been released as of the initial reports.

Dallas police said the investigation is ongoing. The incident follows a series of vehicle-into-pedestrian incidents nationally that have prompted renewed scrutiny of parking lot safety and law enforcement response protocols.

stories.headline: Lakers Defeat Rockets in NBA Playoff Opener Without Doncic or Reaves
stories.summary:
The Los Angeles Lakers defeated the Houston Rockets in Game 1 of their first-round NBA playoff series on Saturday despite being without their top two scorers, Luka Doncic (left hamstring strain) and Austin Reaves (left oblique strain). The Lakers also received an unexpected boost when Rockets star Kevin Durant was ruled out before tipoff with a right knee injury.

Deandre Ayton led Los Angeles with 19 points and 11 rebounds, anchoring the fourth-seeded Lakers to an impressive road win. The victory marked a significant momentum shift for a team that had been viewed as undermanned entering the series.

The win raises questions about Houston's ability to compete in the series with Durant sidelined, and signals that Los Angeles may be a more formidable postseason contender than anticipated given their injury situation. The status of all three injured players heading into Game 2 remains unclear.

stories.headline: Oklahoma Wins NCAA Women's Gymnastics Championship for Fourth Time in Five Years
stories.summary:
The University of Oklahoma Sooners claimed the NCAA women's gymnastics national championship on Saturday, defeating LSU in a closely contested finals. Senior Faith Torrez delivered the decisive performance, nailing a championship-winning floor routine to seal the title for Oklahoma.

The championship marks Oklahoma's fourth national title in five years, cementing the program's status as the dominant force in collegiate women's gymnastics. The Sooners edged out LSU, which had been a perennial contender and defending champion in recent seasons.

The victory underscores the depth and consistency of Oklahoma's gymnastics program, which has built one of the most sustained runs of success in recent NCAA championship history across any sport.
